definition: Covenant-lite loan structures (>90% of leveraged loans) with 15-40% EBITDA addbacks delay the credit channel of monetary policy by 12-24 months by preventing timely defaults, extending the period of above-target inflation and partially explaining observed Phillips Curve flattening.
evidence: 15-40% EBITDA addbacks inflate coverage metrics and delay covenant breaches
Partially explains observed Phillips Curve flattening this cycle
open_questions: How much of apparent Phillips Curve flattening is attributable to covenant-lite delay vs. other factors?
Does the delay create a more severe eventual correction when defaults finally materialize?
